bibliographies: Danish: Kristensen Jyske Folkeminder VII No. 30; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1568A*.
additional_description: The servant girl eats secretly; the miser starves.

name: Deceptive bargain: felling the tree. The ogre and the trickster agree to fell a large tree. The trickster purposely dulls his axe on a stone and then asks the ogre to exchange. Rather than work with a dull axe, the ogre does all the work.
additional_description: The ogre and the trickster agree to fell a large tree. The trickster purposely dulls his axe on a stone and then asks the ogre to exchange. Rather than work with a dull axe, the ogre does all the work.

name: Deceptive bargain: a peck of grain for each stack. The man who is to receive this share of the crop makes very small stacks.
additional_description: The man who is to receive this share of the crop makes very small stacks.

name: Deceptive bargain: an ox for five pennies. A woman who has been left the ox on condition that she give the proceeds to the poor offers it for five pennies, but it must be bought along with a cock at twelve florins. She gives the five pennies to the poor and keeps the twelve florins.
bibliographies: *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 462; *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 188 No. 370; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. *2449.
additional_description: A woman who has been left the ox on condition that she give the proceeds to the poor offers it for five pennies, but it must be bought along with a cock at twelve florins. She gives the five pennies to the poor and keeps the twelve florins.

name: Small niche in house brings large price. House sold reserving niche. This becomes such a nuisance that buyer pays heavily for it.
additional_description: House sold reserving niche. This becomes such a nuisance that buyer pays heavily for it.

name: Deceptive bargain: the ogre and the copper coins. Every time the copper coin is paid out, the ogre must make a new one. The man buys an extensive property and pays with a large number of copper coins. He threatens to buy another and the ogre goes back on his contract.
additional_description: Every time the copper coin is paid out, the ogre must make a new one. The man buys an extensive property and pays with a large number of copper coins. He threatens to buy another and the ogre goes back on his contract.

name: Deceptive land purchase. (Dido.)
additional_description: (Dido.)

name: Deceptive land purchase: ox-hide measure. As much land bought as can be surrounded by an ox-hide. The hide is cut into very small strips.
bibliographies: *Type 2400; *Basset Revue d'ethnographie et des trad. pop. IV 97; Köhler-Bolte II 319ff.; Katanoff "Türkische Sagen über Besitznahme v. Ländern nach Art der Dido" Keleti Szemle III (1902) 173ff.; *Fb "ride" III 52b; Basset RTP VI 335, *VII 549, VIII 409; Rosières RTP VI 52; Sébillot RTP V 186; Cordier RTP II 295, 354. Icelandic: Gering Islendsk Æventyri (Halle, 1883) II 92ff., Herrmann Saxo II 656, Boberg; French: Sébillot France IV 111, 180, 214; Estonian: Loorits, Some Notes on the Repertoire of the Estonian Folk-Tale, Tartu 1937, 23ff.; Greek: Aly Volksmärchen bei Herodot 114, 117; Egyptian: Legrain Louqsor sans les Pharaons (Paris, 1914) 64; N. Am. Indian (Wyandot): Barbeau GSCan XI 271 No. 91.
additional_description: As much land bought as can be surrounded by an ox-hide. The hide is cut into very small strips.

name: Deceptive land purchase: as much land as Vishnu can lie upon (or can step over in three steps). His worshippers claim for him the whole earth.
additional_description: His worshippers claim for him the whole earth.

name: Deceptive land purchase: boundary fixed by flight of a goose. Subject given as much land as a goose can fly over without lighting. The man carries the goose with its wings extended over an enormous territory.
additional_description: Subject given as much land as a goose can fly over without lighting. The man carries the goose with its wings extended over an enormous territory.

name: Deceptive land purchase: as much land as a shawl will cover. An immense shawl prepared.
bibliographies: Harou RTP XXIII 169; Java: Bezemer Fabelen en Legenden 216ff.
additional_description: An immense shawl prepared.

name: Deceptive land purchase: as much land as can be covered by saint's hood. Only by snatching up hood does seller prevent it from covering whole territory.
additional_description: Only by snatching up hood does seller prevent it from covering whole territory.

name: Land grant: as much land as can be covered by saint's mantle.
Deceptive land purchase: bounds fixed by a race. One man has supernatural speed.
additional_description: One man has supernatural speed.

name: Deceptive land purchase: bounds fixed by throwing object (axe, spear). Thrower has extraordinary strength.
additional_description: Thrower has extraordinary strength.

name: Deceptive land purchase: as much land as can be surrounded in a certain time.
Land bargain: land surrounded by a horse (cow) in one day.
bibliographies: Fb "ride" III 52b; Harou RTP XIV 90; India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Oraon : Hahn 94 No. 52 .

name: Land bargain: land ridden around during a sermon.

Deceptive land purchase: king, as reward for help in winning battle, promises wounded chieftain as much land as his chariot can travel around; bribes charioteer to turn back whenever chieftain faints from loss of blood.
Land purchase: as much as can be plowed (fenced) in a certain time.
bibliographies: *Fb "plove" II 850a, "ride" III 52b; cf. Olrik Danske Studier (1910) 4ff.; Icelandic: Hdwb. d. Märchens I 446b nn. 450–454, Mac Culloch Eddic 181, Boberg; Frisian: Lübbing Friesische Sagen 95.

name: Deceptive land purchase: fields that crackle when burnt are to be his. He puts bamboo on the fields before they are burnt so that they crackle.
additional_description: He puts bamboo on the fields before they are burnt so that they crackle.

name: Deceptive land purchase: as much land as can be shadowed by a tree. Bought just before sunset.
bibliographies: Japanese: Ikeda; Indonesia: L. de Backer L'Archipel Indien (Paris, 1874) 334.
additional_description: Bought just before sunset.

name: Deceptive land purchase: enough to raise certain plant. This is a rapidly spreading weed which overruns the country.
bibliographies: Indonesia: Snouck Hurgronje De Atjèhers (Leiden, 1893) II 84.
additional_description: This is a rapidly spreading weed which overruns the country.

name: Deceptive land purchase: saint's enemy promised as much land as he can see from certain point. Saint causes cloud to obstruct vision.
additional_description: Saint causes cloud to obstruct vision.

name: Land grant: as much land as can be seen on a clear day.
Land grant: as much land as can be seen from certain eminence.

Deceptive land bargain: saints agree that the one who casts his staff far enough to reach distant island shall be owner of land. Staff of one contestant transformed to spear (or dart) and so alone reaches island. When saint touches weapon, it becomes staff again.
additional_description: Staff of one contestant transformed to spear (or dart) and so alone reaches island. When saint touches weapon, it becomes staff again.

name: Land grant: as far as ox can be heard.
Deceptive bargain with ogre: buying trees. Trees to be neither straight nor crooked.
additional_description: Trees to be neither straight nor crooked.

name: Strokes shared. The boy promises the soldier what the king has promised to give him. The soldier receives a beating in place of the boy.
bibliographies: *Type 1610; **Reinhard JAFL XXXVI 380; *BP I 62; *Basset 1001 Contes I 321; Köhler-Bolte I 495; *Chauvin V 282 No. 166; *Wesselski Märchen 202 No. 13; *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 614; Hibbard 80 n. 3; Wesselski Mönchslatein 161 No. 122. English: Wells 161 (Sir Cleges); Italian Novella: *Rotunda; India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Venkataswami 106. CENT . INDIA . Twente 47. - MADRAS . Natesa Sastri Tenna . 10 .
additional_description: The boy promises the soldier what the king has promised to give him. The soldier receives a beating in place of the boy.

name: Stealing only a small amount. A man promises in confession to steal only a small amount. He steals a rope with a mare on the end of it.
bibliographies: Spanish: Boggs FFC XC 146 No. 1800A; West Indies: Flowers 498.
additional_description: A man promises in confession to steal only a small amount. He steals a rope with a mare on the end of it.

name: Peace between sheep and wolves. As hostages the dogs are handed over to the wolves; the young wolves to the sheep. The wolves then attack and kill the sheep.
bibliographies: Ward II 320; *Herbert III 168f. No. 22; *Crane Vitry 152 No. 45; Wienert FFC LVI *50 (ET 96), 97 (ST 108); Halm Aesop No. 268; *Krappe Bulletin Hispanique XXXIX 47.
additional_description: As hostages the dogs are handed over to the wolves; the young wolves to the sheep. The wolves then attack and kill the sheep.

name: The man helps the horse against the stag. The horse must agree to be saddled and bridled. The man then refuses to release him.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *71 (ET 356), 108 (ST 208); Halm Aesop No. 175; Herbert III 9; Crane Vitry 182 No. 110; Jacobs Aesop 208 No. 33.
additional_description: The horse must agree to be saddled and bridled. The man then refuses to release him.

name: Deceptive bargain based on an unusual name.
"Old Saddle" granted by the king. This is the name of an estate, which the king unwittingly gives away.
bibliographies: Anderson FFC XLII 360; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. *925.
additional_description: This is the name of an estate, which the king unwittingly gives away.

name: Bargain: if the sun reverses its course. Because of an eclipse the sun is held to have done so, and Atreus becomes king.
additional_description: Because of an eclipse the sun is held to have done so, and Atreus becomes king.

name: A ribbon long enough to reach from ear to ear. The rascal has had an ear cut off and this is in a distant city.
additional_description: The rascal has had an ear cut off and this is in a distant city.

name: Selling by trickery: literal bargain. (Cf. K134.1.)

Buying foxes "as they run". Man sells three hundred foxes to buyer who agrees to "take them as they run": reds, silvers, crosses. He gets a large payment to bind the bargain, waves his hand at the woods: "I sold them as they run; and they're running."
additional_description: Man sells three hundred foxes to buyer who agrees to "take them as they run": reds, silvers, crosses. He gets a large payment to bind the bargain, waves his hand at the woods: "I sold them as they run; and they're running."

name: The tall hog. Man boasts of hog so big that a man could not reach its back if he holds his hand as high as possible. A stranger buys the hog, sight-unseen. The seller takes him to the hog, shows the buyer that the hog's back is much below his hand when he holds it as high as possible.
additional_description: Man boasts of hog so big that a man could not reach its back if he holds his hand as high as possible. A stranger buys the hog, sight-unseen. The seller takes him to the hog, shows the buyer that the hog's back is much below his hand when he holds it as high as possible.

name: Trickster lends bamboo on condition that it is returned exactly as it is.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Orissa : Elwin 4 .

name: Until the log burns out: time given servant for Christmas holidays. Soaks the log so that it burns a week.
additional_description: Soaks the log so that it burns a week.

name: Cheater is forced to eat excrements. Gentleman agrees to exchange his good horse for the peasant's jade, provided the peasant will eat its excrements. The peasant finds no difficulty in the task, whereas the gentleman, put to the same condition when he wants to get back his horse, finds it impossible.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1533*; Russian: Andrejev No. 1529 I*; Chinese: Eberhard FFC CXX 324f. No. 48, FFC CXXVIII 271f. No. 170.
additional_description: Gentleman agrees to exchange his good horse for the peasant's jade, provided the peasant will eat its excrements. The peasant finds no difficulty in the task, whereas the gentleman, put to the same condition when he wants to get back his horse, finds it impossible.

name: Other deceptive bargains.
Deceptive bargain: as much gold in reward as sticks to poet's hair when poured over him: he smears it with tar.
Deception in payment of debt.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K200. Deception in payment of debt.

name: Devil cheated of his promised soul. The man saves it through deceit.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
bibliographies: Irish: Beal XXI 312f., O'Suilleabhain 34, 36; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 3390, Legends Nos. 490f., 749f.; Missouri French: Carrière.
additional_description: The man saves it through deceit.

name: Devil cheated by imposing an impossible task.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil is cheated by giving him task: counting the letters in the church Bible. He is unable to read the holy words.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: He is unable to read the holy words.

name: Devil cheated by being frightened.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
bibliographies: *Type 1145–1154; Irish: Beal XXI 311, O'Suilleabhain 33; India: Thompson-Balys: BENGAL . Bradley - Birt 70f .

name: Man whispers in devil's ear that his wife is approaching with her broom again.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil pounded in knapsack until he releases man.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil's magic power turned on himself. The hero who is riding the devil as a horse receives supernatural strength from plucking a hair from the devil's mane. He then spurs the devil until he agrees to forego his bargain for the man's soul.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The hero who is riding the devil as a horse receives supernatural strength from plucking a hair from the devil's mane. He then spurs the devil until he agrees to forego his bargain for the man's soul.

name: Devil cheated by pretended hanging. The man has promised himself to the devil in return for money. He stuffs his clothes with straw and hangs them up. The devil thinks the man has hanged himself and is satisfied.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The man has promised himself to the devil in return for money. He stuffs his clothes with straw and hangs them up. The devil thinks the man has hanged himself and is satisfied.

name: Devil to release man for performing seemingly impossible task. The task is performed by trickery.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The task is performed by trickery.

name: The evil woman in the glass case as the last commodity. The man is to belong to the devil as soon as he has sold his goods. If he has any goods that no one will buy, he is to be free. The man puts an evil old woman in a glass case. When the devil sees her, he recognizes her. "Whoever knows her will refuse to buy her." The man goes free.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The man is to belong to the devil as soon as he has sold his goods. If he has any goods that no one will buy, he is to be free. The man puts an evil old woman in a glass case. When the devil sees her, he recognizes her. "Whoever knows her will refuse to buy her." The man goes free.

name: Bringing the devil an unknown animal. The man sends his naked wife on all fours in tar and feathers. The devil has never seen such an animal.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
bibliographies: *Type 1091; *BP I 411, III 358; *Fb "pige" II 816a, "kjende" II 140, "tjære" III 811a; Lithuanian: Balys Legends Nos. 755f.
additional_description: The man sends his naked wife on all fours in tar and feathers. The devil has never seen such an animal.

name: Guessing name of devil's secret plant. The man's wife in tar and feathers overhears the devil tell the secret name of the crop he has discovered (tobacco). The devil says to the supposed animal, "Get out of my tobacco!"
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The man's wife in tar and feathers overhears the devil tell the secret name of the crop he has discovered (tobacco). The devil says to the supposed animal, "Get out of my tobacco!"

name: [First Edition (Additions and Corrections): K216.3. Devil's riddles answered: man is freed.]
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Not to sleep for three successive nights. The sleepy man: "I am just thinking, that on earth there are more crooked trees than straight ones ... more hills than plains ... more water than land ..." The devil goes to ascertain these things, meanwhile the man sleeps. Unsuccessful imitation by another man.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The sleepy man: "I am just thinking, that on earth there are more crooked trees than straight ones ... more hills than plains ... more water than land ..." The devil goes to ascertain these things, meanwhile the man sleeps. Unsuccessful imitation by another man.

name: Devil gets another soul instead of one bargained for. The devil bargains with a man for his soul, but the man fulfills his contract and escapes. In envy two persons commit suicide. The devil rejoices that though he lost one he has gained two.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The devil bargains with a man for his soul, but the man fulfills his contract and escapes. In envy two persons commit suicide. The devil rejoices that though he lost one he has gained two.

section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil cheated when his victim becomes a priest.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil cheated of promised soul by intervention of Virgin Mary.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: The picture of the Virgin Mary saves the priest.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 3268, Legends No. 410ff.

name: Penance of priest saves him from devil. The priest, who sold his soul to the devil, orders his servant to cut him (alive) up into pieces, to crucify him on a tree (and the like), thus saves his soul from the devils.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Other ways of cheating the devil of his promised soul.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: Devil cheated of his promised soul when the victim sells his to a comrade. The latter says, "The devil can take only one soul from each person. I bought the soul so that when he comes I can give him one and still save my own."

section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.
additional_description: The devil is to fill a cask full of money. God knocks the bottom out of the cask.

section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K210. Devil cheated of his promised soul.

name: The level bushel. The student is to come into the devil's power if at the end of a year he does not at least return for the heaping bushel of gold a level one. The student forthwith hands back the level bushel and keeps the surplus.

additional_description: In distress a person promises a sacrifice to a god (saint) but disregards the promise when the danger passes.

name: Sailor offers saint a candle as large as a mast. But he knows that after the storm he will not try to find such a candle.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: But he knows that after the storm he will not try to find such a candle.

name: Golden lamb promised to goddess. Common lamb sacrificed.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Common lamb sacrificed.

name: The sacrifice of the cock is at last carried out. Postponed until a hawk carries off the cock. Then the woman says, "O holy St. Martin, I have long owed you a living sacrifice. Take the cock as sacrifice, and may it be pleasing to you."
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Postponed until a hawk carries off the cock. Then the woman says, "O holy St. Martin, I have long owed you a living sacrifice. Take the cock as sacrifice, and may it be pleasing to you."

name: Horse withheld as sacrifice to a saint refuses to move. The deceiver takes the horse to the church planning to remove him again, but the horse will not stir until a money equivalent has been paid.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: The deceiver takes the horse to the church planning to remove him again, but the horse will not stir until a money equivalent has been paid.

name: Sick man offers deity 100 bulls for recovery. When reminded that he does not own so many bulls he explains that he doesn't expect the deity to come to enforce payment.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: When reminded that he does not own so many bulls he explains that he doesn't expect the deity to come to enforce payment.

name: Payment of money to the devil impossible, since debtor learns that the devil is dead.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Debt with worthless bond repudiated.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: A man bonds his loyalty. When the debt is due he offers the creditor his loyalty.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: When the debt is due he offers the creditor his loyalty.

name: Hogs used as a mortgage collateral. (Ground hogs.)
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: (Ground hogs.)

name: False offer to return goods in place of payment.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Milk bought on credit poured into one container. The trickster buys it from various women. After it is all poured together he says that each may have her own back.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: The trickster buys it from various women. After it is all poured together he says that each may have her own back.

name: Order to put a small vessel of milk into huge container. Shrewd group each by himself pours water thinking this will not be detected if the others pour milk.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Shrewd group each by himself pours water thinking this will not be detected if the others pour milk.

name: Trickster gets strong drink by trickery in returning goods.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Trickster returns a bottle of water instead of the bottle of rum he has just purchased.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Trickster fills his gallon jug half full of water, then has it filled with rum at the store. When seller refuses credit, he pours back half gallon of the liquid – now half rum and half water. Sometimes trickster repeats operation, getting richer mixture with each transaction.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: When seller refuses credit, he pours back half gallon of the liquid now half rum and half water. Sometimes trickster repeats operation, getting richer mixture with each transaction.

name: Debtor tells creditor that he has had his reward in the hope of payment.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Toad receives water from frog; refuses earth in return.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Servant refused payment because of single mistake.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Bompas 127 No. 31 .

name: Man refuses to pay murderer for killing and kills him.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Fish promised in return for bacon. Later: "Drink up the river, you shall then have fish. All the fishes there are mine."
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. *1634; Russian: Andrejev No. *2104.
additional_description: Later: "Drink up the river, you shall then have fish. All the fishes there are mine."

name: Debt to be paid "tomorrow". Tomorrow never comes.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Tomorrow never comes.

name: "Come tomorrow". The devil keeps calling daily until the gate with the inscription rots. He then claims his debtor.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1183*; Russian: Andrejev No. 1183*.
additional_description: The devil keeps calling daily until the gate with the inscription rots. He then claims his debtor.

name: Agreement to leave sum of money on coffin of friend. One puts on his share in cash; other makes out a check for the total amount and takes cash left by the other.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: One puts on his share in cash; other makes out a check for the total amount and takes cash left by the other.

name: To pay beggar for standing in tank all night. Beggar sees lights in temple. Payment refused since beggar has thus warmed himself.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Beggar sees lights in temple. Payment refused since beggar has thus warmed himself.

name: Trickster cheats by pretending deafness. Palm rat, when asked to throw down nuts according to bargain, replies that he is deaf when eating.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Palm rat, when asked to throw down nuts according to bargain, replies that he is deaf when eating.

name: Refusal to return borrowed goods.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: Africa (Ekoi): Talbot 68, 375, (Hottentot): Bleek 50 No. 24, (Benga): Nassau 198 No. 29; American Negro (Georgia): Harris Nights 349 No. 61.

name: By using verse with double meaning man appropriates borrowed goods.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: One day and one night: object borrowed for a day and a night retained.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Fairy (god?) loses stronghold by consenting to lend it for "a day and a night."
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: loses stronghold by consenting to lend it for "a day and a night."

name: Trickster escapes without paying.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: KASHMIR . Knowles Tales 348 No. 57. CEY- LON . Parker II 231f .

name: Bird has new clothes made: flies away without paying.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: Spanish: Boggs FFC XC 37 No. 244; India: *Thompson-Balys; Japanese: Ikeda.

name: Drinkers argue about who is to pay. They blindfold the bartender who is to catch one of them; the one who is caught will have to pay. While the bartender is blindfolded, the drinkers leave the tavern.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: They blindfold the bartender who is to catch one of them; the one who is caught will have to pay. While the bartender is blindfolded, the drinkers leave the tavern.

name: Boots made by two cobblers. Trickster sends one of each pair back to be stretched, leaves town with pair of boots made up of the remaining boots.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Trickster sends one of each pair back to be stretched, leaves town with pair of boots made up of the remaining boots.

name: Man orders a bottle of beer, then returns it and takes a loaf of bread instead. He refuses to pay for the bread because he has returned the beer undrunk. He refuses to pay for the beer because he has not drunk it.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: He refuses to pay for the bread because he has returned the beer undrunk. He refuses to pay for the beer because he has not drunk it.

name: Jackal refuses payment for being carried.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Venkataswami 137. - PUNJAB Steel Punjab 230f .; Steel - Temple 244. _ CENT . INDIA . Taylor Folk - Lore VII 87.- CEYLON . Parker I 216 , 347 ; II 386f .

name: Healer to take payment in satisfaction at patient's recovery.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Goods received on partial payment. Buyer refuses to pay more.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Buyer refuses to pay more.

name: Woman promises marriage for pair of earrings: she escapes with them.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Trickster summons all creditors at once, precipitates fight, and escapes payment.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: King promises valuable dog to each of two powerful and mutually hostile tribes. At feast prepared by king the two tribes get into fight and king escapes.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: At feast prepared by king the two tribes get into fight and king escapes.

name: Creditor killed or driven away.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Fox is promised chickens: is driven off by dogs.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Husband promises a cow to tiger; wife frightens the tiger away.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Thor is to give his hammer in return for Freyja as wife. He masks as a woman and kills the giant who is to receive the hammer.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: *DeVries Tijdschrift voor Nederlandsche Taal- en Letterkunde XLVII 293ff.; Icelandic: Boberg.
additional_description: He masks as a woman and kills the giant who is to receive the hammer.

name: Man cheats devil of reward: to have man if he gets him at first grasp. Man holds cat which flies in devil's face. (Cf. K210.)
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Man holds cat which flies in devil's face.

name: Conquered warrior kills victor instead of paying.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Literal payment of debt (not real).
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Fifty ships promised. Forty-nine are moulded out of earth.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Forty-nine are moulded out of earth.

name: Drinking only after a bargain. A woman having thus sworn keeps buying and selling the same mule many times a day.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 306; Scala Celi 81a No. 463; *Crane Vitry 255 No. 277; Herbert III 24.
additional_description: A woman having thus sworn keeps buying and selling the same mule many times a day.

name: Tribute paid in enchanted snow. After payment, snow takes proper form.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: After payment, snow takes proper form.

name: Saint ransoms prince for much gold and silver. Later all the money vanishes. Saint replies that since the money had been created from nothing, it had simply to return to that state.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Later all the money vanishes. Saint replies that since the money had been created from nothing, it had simply to return to that state.

name: Literal fulfillment of marriage contract. Man to be released when earth is placed at his head (buried). Dies in grave.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Man to be released when earth is placed at his head (buried). Dies in grave.

name: Trickster disguises himself and escapes notice of creditors.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Deceptive respite in payment obtained.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Creditor to wait till debtor is shaved. The debtor refuses to finish shaving.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: The debtor refuses to finish shaving.

name: Man who owes 1000 ducats has his creditor arrested for owing him ten. Thus he hopes to postpone payment of his own debt.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Thus he hopes to postpone payment of his own debt.

name: Refusal to tell about the Rhine treasure, though condition demanded is fulfilled when the only one who knows where it is is killed.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: The castration bargain: wife sent. The trickster castrates the dupe and is to come the next day and be castrated himself. He sends his wife as substitute.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: The trickster castrates the dupe and is to come the next day and be castrated himself. He sends his wife as substitute.

name: Creditor falsely reported insane when he demands money.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Arlotto II 225 No. 92; Gonnella 98 No. 2; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1525L*; Italian Novella: Rotunda.

name: King promises beggars new clothes: burns their old and gets much gold and silver. Keeps it.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: Keeps it.

name: Death feigned to avoid paying debts.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: Pierre Faifeu No. 36; India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Grierson Survey IV 508f .

name: Customer takes invitation to buy as invitation to receive the goods free.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Payment evaded by setting countertasks.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Tamil : Robin- son 37 .

name: Deceptions in payment of debt – miscellaneous.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Devil loses his grain and gets thistles. God grants the devil one grain crop, which he can create by calling its name. The devil is tricked into forgetting the name and calling "Thistles". Hence his crop is of thistles.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: God grants the devil one grain crop, which he can create by calling its name. The devil is tricked into forgetting the name and calling "Thistles". Hence his crop is of thistles.

name: Payment of the egg-white. A man dreams of an egg hanging under his bed. An interpreter demands half of what he finds as his fee for interpreting the dream. The man finds that the egg is a silver cup filled with gold crowns. He gives the interpreter part of the cup but none of the gold. The interpreter says, "He gave me some of the egg-white but none of the yolk."
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
additional_description: A man dreams of an egg hanging under his bed. An interpreter demands half of what he finds as his fee for interpreting the dream. The man finds that the egg is a silver cup filled with gold crowns. He gives the interpreter part of the cup but none of the gold. The interpreter says, "He gave me some of the egg-white but none of the yolk."

name: Pseudo-magic money-dropping ass beaten to death by buyer; cheat says: "Return my ass, I shall return your money."
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . NQ II No. 699 .

name: Payment in worthless goods which are alleged to be valuable goods transformed.
division2: K200–K249. Deception in payment of debt.
section: K230. Other deceptions in the payment of debt.

name: Other deceptive bargains.
Deceptive damage claims.

The eaten grain and the cock as damages. A trickster has only a grain of corn; this is eaten by a cock, which he demands and receives as damages. Likewise when a hog eats the cock and the ox eats the hog.
bibliographies: *Type 1655; *BP II 201; *DeVries Volksverhalen II 381 No. 145; India: Thompson-Balys: INDO - BURMESE . Shan : Milne 239 .; American Negro (Georgia): Harris Friends 176 No. 24; Jamaica: Beckwith MAFLS XVII *262 No. 63.
additional_description: A trickster has only a grain of corn; this is eaten by a cock, which he demands and receives as damages. Likewise when a hog eats the cock and the ox eats the hog.

name: Trickster demands return of food guest has just eaten: gets damages.
Damages claimed for loss of a charm. Princess lets trickster's fly (alleged to be a charm) escape.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM HILLS . Sema Naga : Hutton 321 .
additional_description: Princess lets trickster's fly (alleged to be a charm) escape.

name: Damages for supposedly lost horse. Horse concealed by owner in loft of stable at inn.
additional_description: Horse concealed by owner in loft of stable at inn.

name: Damages for accidentally broken water pot: to pay for elephant.
Payment to lame man who claims that man's father lamed him.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: KASHMIR . Jammu : Brown Tawi MS No. 49 .

name: Selling oneself and escaping.
bibliographies: *Type 700; BP I 389; India: Thompson-Balys: U.P . Ghazipur : NQ V No. 70. — INDO - BURMESE . Shan : Milne 269 .

name: Deceptive sale of another as slave.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Grierson Survey III ( 2 ) 403. BENGAL . Banerji 65 ; McCulloch 187ff .

name: Profitable league made with both parties to a quarrel.
Goods misappropriated.
Dog as wolf's shoemaker eats up the materials. Devours the cow, hog, etc. furnished him.
additional_description: Devours the cow, hog, etc. furnished him.

name: Trickster eats sacrifice offerings.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Orissa : Elwin 4

name: Exorbitant price demanded and received.
Charging thirty cakes for cooking twenty-five.
Crab demands seven patas as payment for four patas of paddy frog has borrowed.
Crow demands young swan in payment for helping swan find feed for its young.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P. Crooke - Rouse 150f . No. 35 .

name: Camel has offered one pound of flesh to jackal for help. Camel's tongue demanded.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Swynnerton Raja Rasalu 188 , Upper Indus 34. - U.P. Crooke- Rouse 202 No. 10 .
additional_description: Camel's tongue demanded.

name: Deceptive wages.
Deceptive wages: as much rice as will go on a leaf. Lotus leaf used.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Bompas 125 No. 30 .
additional_description: Lotus leaf used.

name: Deceptive wages: two grains and land to plant them on. Grain multiplies and takes up all of dupe's land.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Bompas 61 No. 16 .
additional_description: Grain multiplies and takes up all of dupe's land.

name: Stolen property sold to its owner.
Trickster steals farmer's cow and then sells her to the farmer.
Merchant buys the same article several times from the same or different seller.
The price of a lump of gold. A trickster asks a goldsmith what he would pay for a lump of gold of a certain size. Believing that the man has such a lump, the goldsmith pays him a large sum.
additional_description: A trickster asks a goldsmith what he would pay for a lump of gold of a certain size. Believing that the man has such a lump, the goldsmith pays him a large sum.

name: The price of mink skins. Man asks peddler what he pays for mink skins. Peddler says he will pay ten dollars. The man tosses a skin into the cart, receives ten dollars. The next day the peddler protests that the man has sold him a cat skin. The man says that he had not said that the skin was a mink skin and that, anyway, the cat's name had been "Mink."
additional_description: Man asks peddler what he pays for mink skins. Peddler says he will pay ten dollars. The man tosses a skin into the cart, receives ten dollars. The next day the peddler protests that the man has sold him a cat skin. The man says that he had not said that the skin was a mink skin and that, anyway, the cat's name had been "Mink."

name: The priest made sick of his bargain: three words at the grave. A poor man in return for a steer gets permission from the priest to speak three words at the burial of his enemy, the rich man. Priest: "From earth are you come." Man: "Now the steer is dead." Priest: "In earth shall you remain." Man: "Father, do you want the meat?" Priest: "I wish you were in hell!" etc.
additional_description: A poor man in return for a steer gets permission from the priest to speak three words at the burial of his enemy, the rich man. Priest: "From earth are you come." Man: "Now the steer is dead." Priest: "In earth shall you remain." Man: "Father, do you want the meat?" Priest: "I wish you were in hell!" etc.

name: Devil is made sick of his bargain. Devil helps shepherd boy become a minister on condition that he mention Satan by name each time he enters pulpit. Boy consents but does so in such a way that devil begs to abolish the agreement.
additional_description: Devil helps shepherd boy become a minister on condition that he mention Satan by name each time he enters pulpit. Boy consents but does so in such a way that devil begs to abolish the agreement.

name: Agreement not to scratch. In talking the trickster makes gestures and scratches without detection.
bibliographies: *Bolte Zs. f. Vksk. XIX 310 n. 2; North Carolina: Brown Collection I 701; India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Twente 47f .; American Negro (Georgia): Harris Nights 214 No. 37, (South Carolina): Parsons JAFL XXXVIII 218; Jamaica: Beckwith MAFLS XVII 36 No. 29; West Indies: Flowers 502.
additional_description: In talking the trickster makes gestures and scratches without detection.

name: Deceptive wager.
Deceptive wager: whose horse will jump highest. The trickster has his worthless horse jump out the window. The duke will not let his run the risk.
additional_description: The trickster has his worthless horse jump out the window. The duke will not let his run the risk.

name: Deceptive wager: cat to carry lantern into room. (Has been specially trained.)
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDO - TIBETAN . O'Connor No. 6 .
additional_description: (Has been specially trained.)

name: The fee used up before the main question is reached. A man with an unsolved question seeks the help of a wit. The latter refuses to answer unless paid. He takes small fees for each easy question leading up to the principal question. Before reaching that point the fee is exhausted, and the question remains unanswered.
additional_description: A man with an unsolved question seeks the help of a wit. The latter refuses to answer unless paid. He takes small fees for each easy question leading up to the principal question. Before reaching that point the fee is exhausted, and the question remains unanswered.

name: New bags for old! Recovery of the old bag (containing money or having magic power) which the stupid wife has given away. The husband exchanges a new bag for it.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 201 No. 393; Chauvin V 64 n. 1.
additional_description: Recovery of the old bag (containing money or having magic power) which the stupid wife has given away. The husband exchanges a new bag for it.

name: Counting out pay. Hole in the hat and hat over a pit.
additional_description: Hole in the hat and hat over a pit.

name: Dupe denied food until hands are clean. Grass burned around food makes continued washings unavailing.
bibliographies: Africa (Zezuru): Posselt Fables of the Veld (London, 1929) 110 (Northern Rhodesia): Worthington The Little Wise One (London, 1930) 25ff., (Nyanja): Rattray Some Folk-Lore Stories ... in Chinyanja (London, 1907) 145f. No. 22, (Namwanga): Dewar Chinamwanga Stories (Livingstonia, 1900) 47, (Fang): Anthropos XXVIII 292 No. 7, (Bulu): Krug JAFL XXV 114, (Mossi, Nioniossee, Samo, Yarse, Silmi-Mossi, Fulah): Tauxier Le Noir du Yatenga (Paris, 1917) 458f. No. 59.
additional_description: Grass burned around food makes continued washings unavailing.

name: Trickster sells what is not his to sell.

Man contracts for load of hay on the road (without making any payment), orders the seller to deliver it at a certain inn. He then goes to the inn, sells the hay to the innkeeper, and pockets the money. The owner of the hay delivers it at the inn, tries to collect at the inn; the trickster absconds.
additional_description: He then goes to the inn, sells the hay to the innkeeper, and pockets the money. The owner of the hay delivers it at the inn, tries to collect at the inn; the trickster absconds.

name: Other means of theft.
Theft by disobeying orders: misappropriation.
Jackal ordered to take meat to lion's family takes it to his own.
Man calling self "her husband" abducts child.

name: Tricksters eat up food given them for bear. Escape.

name: Monk entrusted with care of getting husband for girl, takes dowry himself.
Uncle entrusted with niece's patrimony slanders her so marriage will be broken off and he will not have to part with her money.
Covetous husband desiring wife's jewels tells her he has vowed to offer them to deity.
Theft by presenting false order to guardian.

name: Unique weapon got by misrepresenting to guardian use to which it is to be put.
For the long winter. The numskull has been told to keep the sausage "for the long winter". When the trickster hears this, he claims to be Long Winter and receives the sausage.

additional_description: The numskull has been told to keep the sausage "for the long winter". When the trickster hears this, he claims to be Long Winter and receives the sausage.

name: Ring to put on corpse's finger. A thief holds a corpse up to a lord's window. The lord shoots the corpse and leaves to bury it. The thief goes to the lady and gets a sheet to bury the corpse in and a ring to put on his finger.
bibliographies: *Type 1525A; BP III 379; Missouri French: Carrière; Jewish: Neuman.
additional_description: A thief holds a corpse up to a lord's window. The lord shoots the corpse and leaves to bury it. The thief goes to the lady and gets a sheet to bury the corpse in and a ring to put on his finger.

name: The cup to be repaired. A thief poses as a messenger from a husband to his wife asking that a certain silver cup be sent for repairs.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Mönchslatein 126 No. 109; Scala Celi 101a No. 543; Mensa Philosophica No. 56; Italian Novella: Rotunda.
additional_description: A thief poses as a messenger from a husband to his wife asking that a certain silver cup be sent for repairs.

name: Stealing the platter for the intended gift. Thief asks for silver platter saying that it is needed for an intended gift of confections. Servant carrying platter is told to wait until it is filled. Thief disappears with it.
additional_description: Thief asks for silver platter saying that it is needed for an intended gift of confections. Servant carrying platter is told to wait until it is filled. Thief disappears with it.

name: Hare in lion's skin gets meat from lioness.

name: Deposit money secured by false order to banker's wife. When banker refuses to redeliver deposit the owner presents false tokens to wife.

name: Theft by forgery: signature forged to obtain money.

name: Clerk mistranslates order given by master to maid, so that pie goes to clerks.

name: Thieving cow.
Cow makes a hundred-fold return. The trickster has a cow that leads the parson's cows to him. He thus tests the parson's text, "He who gives in God's name shall have it back a hundred-fold."

additional_description: Brothers are given the choice of a cow or a calf. One chooses the cow and thinks that he has the best of the bargain. The cow longs for the calf and returns to it.

name: Animal shams death and is sold. Returns to master.

name: "Owner has refused to accept it." A rascal steals a priest's watch. He tells the priest that he has stolen a watch and offers it to him as a payment for a past favor. The priest refuses to accept stolen goods. Commands the thief to return the watch to the owner. "But the owner has refused to accept it." "Then you may keep it."
additional_description: A rascal steals a priest's watch. He tells the priest that he has stolen a watch and offers it to him as a payment for a past favor. The priest refuses to accept stolen goods. Commands the thief to return the watch to the owner. "But the owner has refused to accept it." "Then you may keep it."

Thief successfully accuses owner of having stolen property he covets.

bibliographies: *Type 785; *BP II 149, 153; Wienert FFC LVI 40, 107; Oesterley No. 83; Herbert III 205; *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 57; Penzer V 130 n. 1; India: Thompson-Balys: KASHMIR . Jammu : Brown Tawi MS No. 27. - PUNJAB . Ondh : Crooke Ind . Antiq . XXXV 147 No. 10 .

name: The goose without a leg. Accused of eating the goose's leg, the thief maintains that it had no leg, and cleverly enforces his point by showing geese standing on one leg. (Usually the master confounds the rascal by frightening the geese so that they use both legs)

name: The bird without a tail. Thief maintains that the bird had no tail.
additional_description: Thief maintains that the bird had no tail.

name: The ass without a heart. The ass as toll-gatherer is killed by the lion for asking for toll. The fox eats the ass's heart. When the lion asks for it, the fox replies that the ass could have had no heart since he was such a fool as to ask the lion for toll.
bibliographies: **Keidel "Die Eselherz Fabel" Zs. f. vgl. Litgsch. n. ser. VII No. 58; Gaster Exempla 229 No. 244; Penzer V 130 n. 1; *Chauvin II 99 No. 58; Bødker Exempler 299 No. 63; Spanish Exempla: Keller; Jewish: *Neuman; India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Swynnerton Upper Indus 269 ; CEYLON .

name: Thief claims to have been transformed into an ass. While the owner sleeps the thief steals his horse, hitches himself to the wagon, and claims that he is the horse transformed into a man.
bibliographies: *Type 1529; *BP III 9, 391 n. 3; Chauvin VII 137; *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 229 No. 487; *Basset 1001 Contes I 492; Spanish: Boggs FFC XC 150 No. 1852*; Philippine: Fansler MAFLS XII 437.

additional_description: The thief gets a receipt from the owner and thus defends himself when accused of theft.

name: Thief successfully claims that stolen image has been given him by the saint himself.

name: Stolen animal magically transformed so that thief may escape detection.

additional_description: (Previously he had severed father's or brother's head to escape detection.)

name: The priest as surety. Feasters are imprisoned because of failure to pay for the food. They name the priest as surety and are released. The priest has been told that the host is possessed and agrees to come to heal him in two weeks. The host loses the money.
additional_description: Feasters are imprisoned because of failure to pay for the food. They name the priest as surety and are released. The priest has been told that the host is possessed and agrees to come to heal him in two weeks. The host loses the money.

name: Complaint about the empty bottle. While the servant in the inn is bringing a glass, the trickster drinks the wine and then complains that he has been given an empty bottle. The servant must bring another.
additional_description: While the servant in the inn is bringing a glass, the trickster drinks the wine and then complains that he has been given an empty bottle. The servant must bring another.

name: Greatest liar to get his supper free. Wager. Each lie is corroborated by a confederate, who poses as a newly arrived stranger.
additional_description: Wager. Each lie is corroborated by a confederate, who poses as a newly arrived stranger.

name: Credit based on forgery.
Forged letter used to obtain credit, consideration, and entertainment.
Forged testament used to dupe host.
Worthless chests offered to obtain credit.
Trickster receives huge sum on trifling credit by chain of borrowings. Pays small sum in advance for first sum borrowed. Pays this borrowed sum in advance for larger, etc.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Bompas 162 No. 49 .
additional_description: Pays small sum in advance for first sum borrowed. Pays this borrowed sum in advance for larger, etc.

name: Trickster takes goods given in charity to his family.
The bear helps the fox's mother get berries: the fox eats them.
Monkey causes girl to cry as if from hunger: eats food given her.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM HILLS . Rengma Naga : Mills 264 .

name: Eavesdropping sexton duped into giving suppliant money. The trickster prays to the Virgin for a certain sum of money and promises repayment of double at the end of the month. The sexton throws the money to him, but never receives it back.
additional_description: The trickster prays to the Virgin for a certain sum of money and promises repayment of double at the end of the month. The sexton throws the money to him, but never receives it back.

name: Owner bids on his own goods at auction. Rival buyer pays extravagant price.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 204 No. 405; Chauvin VIII 107 No. 83.
additional_description: Rival buyer pays extravagant price.

name: The substituted porridge. In cooking dinner fox's porridge is light, bear's black. At dinner fox steals spoonful of bear's porridge and lets bear taste it. Bear believes that fox's porridge is as bad as his own.
bibliographies: *Type 9 C; Dh IV 249ff.; Krohn Bär (Wolf) und Fuchs (JSFO VI) 97ff.
additional_description: In cooking dinner fox's porridge is light, bear's black. At dinner fox steals spoonful of bear's porridge and lets bear taste it. Bear believes that fox's porridge is as bad as his own.

name: Sham blood and brains. Fox covers his head with milk and says that his brains have been knocked out. Frightens bear.
bibliographies: *Type 3; Dh IV 243; Krohn Bar (Wolf) und Fuchs (JSFO VI) 59ff.; Japanese: Ikeda; Indonesia: DeVries's list No. 287; American Negro (Georgia): Harris Nights 21 No. 5, 377 No. 68.
additional_description: Fox covers his head with milk and says that his brains have been knocked out. Frightens bear.

name: Trickster cheats rescuers into digging his well. The well that he has dug falls in. He throws his clothes into the hole and hides. People going to church think that man is drowned and dig the well out.
additional_description: The well that he has dug falls in. He throws his clothes into the hole and hides. People going to church think that man is drowned and dig the well out.

name: Cheating through equivocation.
bibliographies: Köhler-Bolte I 513; Breton: Sébillot Incidents s. v. "équivoque"; India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Maria : Rao , R. 21

name: The stolen meat handed about. The thief hands it to his confederate. He says, "I haven't it." The confederate says, "I didn't steal it."
additional_description: The thief hands it to his confederate. He says, "I haven't it." The confederate says, "I didn't steal it."

name: "Have we leave to go?" Two prisoners are made stable boys on their promise not to escape secretly. Before horse race starts they ask: "Do we have your leave to go?" They go home.
additional_description: Two prisoners are made stable boys on their promise not to escape secretly. Before horse race starts they ask: "Do we have your leave to go?" They go home.

name: Why go all the way to fair? Man robbed of his plate of cakes half way to fair asks another vendor, "Why go to the fair, when half way up people come demanding your plate?" Vendor goes on and meets with same fate.
additional_description: Man robbed of his plate of cakes half way to fair asks another vendor, "Why go to the fair, when half way up people come demanding your plate?" Vendor goes on and meets with same fate.

name: Cheating by substitution of worthless articles.
Entrails substituted for meat. Prometheus divides slain ox so that bones and entrails seem to be choicest part. (Zeus is not deceived.)
additional_description: Prometheus divides slain ox so that bones and entrails seem to be choicest part. (Zeus is not deceived.)

name: Rock substituted for ham by trickster.
Tortoise cheats leopard by substituting bundle of resin for bundle of meat.
False articles used to produce credit.
Nugget of supposed gold (lead) given to help build church: money then borrowed.
Reward for the bag of lead. A man sews up lead in a bag and feigns to have found it. A merchant claims it and thinking it filled with gold pays him a large reward.
additional_description: A man sews up lead in a bag and feigns to have found it. A merchant claims it and thinking it filled with gold pays him a large reward.

name: Water sold as wine. Wine-casks partitioned: one half wine, other half water.
additional_description: Wine-casks partitioned: one half wine, other half water.

name: Innkeeper serves sweetened water for wine, cat for rabbit, mule for beef.
False set of rings to offset genuine. Jewels bought with counterfeit money.
additional_description: Jewels bought with counterfeit money.

name: Priests substitute gilded images of calves for those of solid gold.
While swimming with the lizard, toad exchanges own ugly daughter for lizard's pretty one.
Lean geese substituted for fat by trickster.
Woman gives friend dried comb while she herself eats the honey.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Orissa : Elwin .

name: Cheating by substitution of common cow for magic one.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Mitra , S. , Man in India VIII 214f .

name: Attention secured by trickery.

Audience secured with the pope by rudeness. A woman bribes a man to get her an audience with the pope. By turning his back to the sacrament and saying that the woman had instructed him to do so, he brings it about that she is summoned into the presence.
additional_description: A woman bribes a man to get her an audience with the pope. By turning his back to the sacrament and saying that the woman had instructed him to do so, he brings it about that she is summoned into the presence.

name: Deception into listening to speaker. He secures the audience's attention by beginning a tale. He then launches into his speech.
bibliographies: Wesselski Mönchslatein 74 No. 64; Wienert FFC LVI 38; Halm Aesop Nos 177, 339.
additional_description: He secures the audience's attention by beginning a tale. He then launches into his speech.

name: Entry into enemy's presence by pretending to be a messenger from a relative.
Butter weighed with the bread. The peasant weighs the butter which he is selling to the baker along with the bread which he is buying.
additional_description: The peasant weighs the butter which he is selling to the baker along with the bread which he is buying.

name: Demi-coq by means of his magic animals and magic water collects money.
Blackbird to avenge capture of his wife carries rope, club, cat, ants and river in ears.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P. Crooke - Rouse 210 No. 28 .

name: Money received to bury sham-dead person.
Husband and wife each receive money (from different persons) to bury the other, who is supposed to be dead.
Trickster reports treasure's owner dead: receives it from children.
Color of devil's cows changed while he sleeps so that he does not know them. Only those not changed (all black, all red, etc.) belong to the devil.
bibliographies: Dh I 188; Lithuanian: Balys Legends No. 134.
additional_description: Only those not changed (all black, all red, etc.) belong to the devil.

name: Cheating by raising an alarm.
Trickster gets money from a bank by raising an alarm and demanding "what is owing to him."
Host with overstock of sour wine spreads rumor of dragon at his house. A crowd gathers and he sells all his wine.
additional_description: A crowd gathers and he sells all his wine.

name: False alarm of robbery causes cheated man to be imprisoned.
bibliographies: Boccaccio Decameron IX No. 4; Italian Novella: Rotunda; Persian: Lorimer Persian Tales (London, 1919) 321 No. 52.

name: The devil gets into the ark. The devil wants to know what Noah is doing when he is building the ark. He forbids Noah's wife to enter the ark until Noah has also invited him.
bibliographies: *Type 825; *Dh I 258; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 3100, Legends Nos. 192–195.
additional_description: The devil wants to know what Noah is doing when he is building the ark. He forbids Noah's wife to enter the ark until Noah has also invited him.

name: The double-cheating miller. He confesses that he has an oversized measure and agrees to get a smaller one. He measures back the grain in the smaller measure.
additional_description: He confesses that he has an oversized measure and agrees to get a smaller one. He measures back the grain in the smaller measure.

name: Counselor accuses conspirators in order to confiscate their estates.
Lawyer's dog steals meat. The lawyer tells the butcher that the dog's owner (himself) is liable for damages. He ask double the amount of the damages as fee.
additional_description: The lawyer tells the butcher that the dog's owner (himself) is liable for damages. He ask double the amount of the damages as fee.

name: Trickster paid to educate an ass. He gets paid in advance. He gradually starves the ass.
bibliographies: *Type 1675; *BP I 59; *Bolte Zs. f. Vksk. VII 93; *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 247f. No. 552; India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Bannu : Thorburn 209
additional_description: He gets paid in advance. He gradually starves the ass.

name: Trickster paid to teach monkey to talk.
Horse to be taught to speak.
Girl serves her father with piece of her own flesh in place of chicken.
bibliographies: Spanish: Boggs FFC XC 125 No. 1374B; India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Baiga : Elwin 508 .

name: Woman serves beggar with coals instead of food.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Orissa : Elwin

name: Dupe betrayed by asking him ambiguous questions. They are phrased in such a way that he understands them differently from the way they are intended.
additional_description: They are phrased in such a way that he understands them differently from the way they are intended.

name: Wolf announces dawn prematurely to collect debt. The contract is to be fulfilled at daybreak. The wolf imitates the cock and crows, but is caught.
bibliographies: Cape Verde Islands: *Parsons MAFLS XV (1) 6 n. 1.
additional_description: The contract is to be fulfilled at daybreak. The wolf imitates the cock and crows, but is caught.

name: Trickster shams sickness so that partner does all the work.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Santal : Bompas 457 No. 5

name: Foxes persuade man to plant cooked plants. They eat them at night.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM VALLEY . Barooah 72f . No. 9. - ASSAM HILLS . Lushai : Parry 550
additional_description: They eat them at night.

name: Persons deceived into eating meat in Lent, the meat being disguised as butter. (Cf. K499.2.1, K499.2.2.)
Additional cheats.
Trickster sells mother's wine to merchant without asking her permission. Mother saves part of wine because purchaser is dilatory in removing casks.
additional_description: Mother saves part of wine because purchaser is dilatory in removing casks.

name: Object with a hollow as instrument of cheats.
Saint who desires broth containing no butter receives broth into which butter has been poured surreptitiously through hollow mixing-stick. (Cf. K498.)

Saint who desires pottage of nettles containing no milk receives pottage into which milk has been poured surreptitiously through pipe. The secret is revealed, and the saint blesses the cook's successors.
additional_description: The secret is revealed, and the saint blesses the cook's successors.

name: Old man cheats crocodile by playing on its ignorance of agriculture.
Trickster breaks cat of taste for milk by overheating its milk.
Embarrassing gift. Trickster unwilling to pay for burial fee of aged cow, gives cow to unwitting Brahmin as gift. Cow dies soon and Brahmin must pay.
additional_description: Trickster unwilling to pay for burial fee of aged cow, gives cow to unwitting Brahmin as gift. Cow dies soon and Brahmin must pay.

name: God cheats birds by giving false description of tamarind fruit.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Orissa : Elwin .

name: Gullible king gives large sums to minister for construction of imaginary weapon and then more to have it destroyed.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama - Shankar lllf .

name: Trickster dupes rival by exchanging beds: receives his food. Overhears maiden tell rival she will bring food at night, waits until rival is asleep and carries him to another bed, takes his place, and is fed by maiden.
additional_description: Overhears maiden tell rival she will bring food at night, waits until rival is asleep and carries him to another bed, takes his place, and is fed by maiden.

name: Treacherous friend drinks out of other's flask to save the water in his own.
Fox pretends to go to work, but goes out to sleep.
bibliographies: S. Am. Indian (Chiriguano): Métraux RMLP XXXIII 175.

name: Escape from death or danger by deception.
section: K500. Escape from death or danger by deception.

name: Death order evaded.

additional_description: Poet (who recognizes the meaning) reports to king that inscription means a request for honorable treatment.

Compassionate executioners. Slaves charged with killing (drowning) the infant heroine are touched by her "laughing smile" and put her in a calfshed (hollow tree), where she is found by cowherds, who rear her.

additional_description: Executioner persuaded to let hero go.

Compassionate executioner: substituted puppet drowned.

name: Let me live as long as this candle lasts. Man who has sold his soul to devil thus escapes. (Cf. G303.12.5.4.)
additional_description: Man who has sold his soul to devil thus escapes.

name: Escape by asking a last kiss. Uses the opportunity to attack adversary.
additional_description: Uses the opportunity to attack adversary.

name: Ten (five) year respite given captive while he undertakes to teach elephant (ass) to speak. Captive explains to friends that in that time the captor, the elephant (ass), or himself is likely to die.
additional_description: Captive explains to friends that in that time the captor, the elephant (ass), or himself is likely to die.

name: Respite from death until muddy victim may dry self in sun.

name: Respite from death until visit is finished.

name: Respite from death until prisoner is healed by magic object.

name: Respite from death for drink of water.

additional_description: Wolf kicked to death.

name: Respite from death granted while wolf counts hairs in horse's tail. Wolf kicked to death.
additional_description: Wolf kicked to death.

name: Wolf is requested by tailor to be measured for suit of clothes; wolf beaten.
Respite from death until minister shows king how to reap pearls.
Definite respite from death granted.
A year's time granted to settle affairs before death.
God grants man twenty years more of life provided he plays no tricks.
Crocodile grants boy five days respite from death.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Natesa Sastri Folklore 8 .

name: Escape by false plea: jackal asks to be able to clasp tree before crocodile kills it.

name: The cave call. ("Hello, house!") An animal suspecting the presence of an enemy in his cave (house), calls and receives no answer. He then says, "Don't you know, O cave, that we have agreed that I must call you when I come from abroad and that you in turn must answer me?" The hiding animal answers and the other flees.

additional_description: A father sends his sons one after the other to pasture the goat. The goat always declares that he has had nothing to eat. The father angrily sends his sons from home and learns, when he himself tries to pasture the goat, that he has been deceived.

name: Casual words uttered by dupe used to cheat him of his property. A miser is persuaded by his servant to fast nine days. He calls out on the fifth day "the half" and on the ninth "the whole". She makes people believe that he is making his will and giving everything to her. It is so ordered.
section: K1110. Deceptions into self injury – miscellaneous.
bibliographies: Danish: Kristensen Jyske Folkeminder VII No. 30.
additional_description: A miser is persuaded by his servant to fast nine days. He calls out on the fifth day "the half" and on the ninth "the whole". She makes people believe that he is making his will and giving everything to her. It is so ordered.

section: K1110. Deceptions into self injury – miscellaneous.

name: Vergil in the basket. A lover who is to be pulled up to his mistress's window is left hanging in the basket in the public gaze.

name: Lover caught in roof.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P. Oudh : Crooke Ind . Antiq . XXXV 144 .

name: Lover left standing in snow while his mistress is with another. He later tricks her into standing a whole day in July in a tower naked exposed to the sun and flies.
bibliographies: Boccaccio Decameron VIII No. 7 (*Lee 258); Italian Novella: Rotunda.
additional_description: He later tricks her into standing a whole day in July in a tower naked exposed to the sun and flies.

name: Terrorizing the paramour (importunate lover).

Woman dares husband to try his sword on pile of clothing which hides her paramour. Stops him just in time. Later the paramour entices her to come to him. Exposes her naked, except for face, to his friends.
additional_description: Stops him just in time. Later the paramour entices her to come to him. Exposes her naked, except for face, to his friends.

name: Adulteress frightens paramour with cries of "Rape!" Then she removes her husband's suspicion by feigning a fit. Later the paramour tricks her to his room and shows her naked, except for face, to her husband.
additional_description: " Then she removes her husband's suspicion by feigning a fit. Later the paramour tricks her to his room and shows her naked, except for face, to her husband.

name: Prostitute frightens lover with cries of "Thief!" Gets his money.
additional_description: " Gets his money.

name: Hercules spins for his beloved. Is forced to dress as woman and discharge womanly duties including spinning.
additional_description: Is forced to dress as woman and discharge womanly duties including spinning.

name: Importunate lover induced to dress as woman and sift flour. Is shown to his wife.
bibliographies: Italian Novella: *Rotunda; Heptameron No. 69; Cent Nouvelles Nouvelles No. 17.
additional_description: Is shown to his wife.

name: Importunate lover is induced to undergo series of humiliations. (1) Disguise as bakery woman. (2) Disguise as priest. (3) Disguise as corpse. (4) Hiding in wine skin. Humiliated each time.
additional_description: (1) Disguise as bakery woman. (2) Disguise as priest. (3) Disguise as corpse. (4) Hiding in wine skin. Humiliated each time.

additional_description: He hides in a chest. The chest is taken by a creditor who unwittingly delivers it to the paramour's wife.

name: Importunate lovers led astray.
The entrapped suitors. (Lai l'épervier.) The chaste wife has them one at a time undress and hide. The husband and guests come and chase them off.
